$note: Work-type pipelines (declarative). The orchestrator reads this: parse `sdlc <type> <slug>` (default type=feature), then run the type's `phases` IN ORDER, using each phase's agent + gate from the shared catalogs below. Phases, gates, and OpenSpec lifecycle are defined ONCE (reused by every type); each type only references them + sets delta mode and overrides. Edit per project to tune which phases a type runs.

lifecycle.implement: /opsx:apply   (or the openspec-apply skill — loops tasks.md checkboxes)
lifecycle.archive: openspec archive "<change-name>"   (merges spec deltas into openspec/specs/)

S2.produces: AC scenarios + Early Risk Flags + stride

gateCatalog.SPEC_LOCK: spec-auditor PASS  AND  openspec change validate passes
gateCatalog.DESIGN_REVIEW: cross-artifact-audit 0 CRITICAL  AND  openspec change validate passes
gateCatalog.QA: 0 Critical/High bugs open; all ACs verified
gateCatalog.RELEASE: migration reviewed + rollback plan + 30-min post-deploy stable

$rigor: Each type carries a `rigor` field = the FLOOR the orchestrator may resolve to. 'full' = eligible for the convergence loop + .xlsx test cases (heavy, slow — for important / business-logic-tight work). 'lite' = single-pass audits + markdown test cases (fast — small features, bugfix, hotfix). 'lite' is a HARD floor: a type marked lite never runs full, even if sdlc.config gates.convergence=always. Resolution order (orchestrator): (1) runtime flag --rigor=full|lite on the sdlc command; (2) this type floor (lite types stay lite); (3) sdlc.config gates.convergence: always→full, never→lite, auto→ASK the user once at kickoff (full vs lite). The chosen rigor is persisted to _state.json.rigor so later sessions don't re-ask.

rebuild.desc: Re-implement existing behavior. Like feature, but read the existing source for parity first.

cr.desc: Change request to an existing capability. Spec delta uses MODIFIED requirements.
cr.note: S3 only if the change alters design; otherwise skip straight to S4.

bugfix.desc: Fast-track: clear root cause, no design change. Skip S1-S3.

gateOverrides.S5: regression-only (retest + regression scope; no full test design)

bugfix.note: Orchestrator creates the isolation branch/worktree (git.isolation) before S4 — e.g. `bugfix/<ticket>-<slug>`. Still `openspec new change` with a short proposal + a spec delta ONLY if behavior changes. Escalate to full feature if scope grows.

hotfix.desc: Emergency production fix. Minimal build + smoke test, deploy fast.

gateOverrides.S4: minimal — fix + a regression test for the incident only

hotfix.note: Orchestrator creates the isolation branch/worktree (git.isolation) before S4 — `hotfix/<ticket>-<slug>`. Backfill spec delta + S5 retroactively after the incident if behavior changed.

spike.desc: Research/exploration — produces a decision doc, not code. Use when 'should we build X?' is still open.

gateOverrides.S2: decision-doc.md complete + recommendation clear (GO build / NO-GO / defer); no code artifacts required

spike.note: Output: proposal.md + decision-doc.md (replaces design.md — write it as: Problem, Options Considered, Decision, Consequences). No S3 onwards. Escalate to `feature` if the spike concludes GO.

tech-debt.desc: Refactor or cleanup — zero behavior change. S3 confirms scope before any code is touched.

gateOverrides.S3: design.md MUST contain a 'Zero-Behavior-Change Confirmation' section listing what will NOT change; no new ACs introduced
gateOverrides.S5: regression-only: retest affected area + run full suite; no new test scenarios, no test design phase

tech-debt.note: No S1/S2 — the behavior is already specified in the living spec. S3 MUST confirm zero-behavior-change before S4 starts. If behavior must change to fix a design flaw, escalate to `cr`.
